name: Optimus Futures
description_short: Independent futures broker offering 100+ markets, zero platform fees, ultra-low commissions from $0.25/side, and simultaneous access to multiple professional trading platforms.
description_long:
Optimus Futures is an independent introducing broker (IB) founded in 2004 and registered with the NFA, specializing exclusively in futures and options on futures trading. Based in Boca Raton, Florida, Optimus provides access to over 100 futures markets spanning equity indices, metals, energies, agricultural commodities, bonds, currencies, and crypto futures across major exchanges including CME, CBOT, NYMEX, ICE, Eurex, Euronext, and Osaka.

One of Optimus Futures' strongest differentiators is its multi-platform approach. Traders get free access to Optimus Flow (a proprietary desktop platform with DOM and volume analysis tools), Optimus Web, and Optimus Mobile — and can simultaneously connect to third-party platforms including TradingView, MetaTrader 5, Sierra Chart, MultiCharts, Quantower, and Bookmap. This flexibility allows traders to use their preferred tools while routing all orders through a single account.

Pricing is highly competitive, with micro futures commissions starting at just $0.25/side plus exchange fees, and standard contracts from $0.75/side. There are no monthly platform fees or inactivity charges, and CME Level 1 market data is free for traders executing 10 or more contracts per month. Minimum deposits start at $500 for micro accounts, and day trading margins are among the lowest in the industry — as little as $50 for the Micro E-mini S&P.

Optimus Futures has been recognized as Benzinga's Best Software for Trading Futures for three consecutive years (2023, 2024, 2025). The firm supports algorithmic and automated trading through Rithmic and CQG APIs, and has recently expanded its feature set with integrated trade journaling, multi-bracket orders, and CME Group event contracts.

tiers.name: Micro Account
features: Free Optimus Flow, Web, and Mobile platforms
CME Level 1 data free with 10+ trades/month

tiers.name: Standard Account
features: Full access to all supported third-party platforms
API access via Rithmic and CQG

pros: Zero platform fees with free access to multiple professional platforms simultaneously
Ultra-low commissions starting at $0.25/side for micro futures contracts
Supports 8+ third-party platforms including TradingView, Sierra Chart, and Bookmap
Benzinga Best Futures Software award winner three consecutive years (2023–2025)
Low $500 minimum deposit with day trading margins as low as $50
cons: No spot forex, equities, or equity options — futures and options on futures only
Wire withdrawal fees reportedly higher than industry average
No built-in backtesting on proprietary platforms — requires third-party tools
